layer_map.what: The `Tabs` primitive — hand-rolled `role="tablist"` strip rendering tab buttons from a flat descriptor array; controlled via `activeId`/`onChange`; selection-only (renders no panels); optional right-aligned `actions` slot
Sibling token-bound stylesheet; semantic BEM classes bound to `tokens.css`; no inline styles; `prefers-reduced-motion` guard
Reused `cx()` className composer for BEM assembly (no change)
`Icon` atom available for a future per-tab leading icon; not required by the in-scope ACs (reuse only if added)
Register `Tabs` for manual visual fidelity check vs `design/reference.html`
Vitest + Testing Library interaction tests; Playwright CT keyboard/focus tests

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Hand-roll `role="tablist"`/`role="tab"` buttons with manual roving tabindex + Arrow/Home/End/wrap + disabled-skip
key_design_decisions.why: **DEPARTURE: diverges from the Dropdown/Modal/Toast "wrap Radix" a11y precedent — justified because Radix `Tabs.Trigger` deterministically emits `aria-controls` to a sibling `Tabs.Content` (Context7-confirmed in research.md); with no Content mounted (selection-only, §6) that attribute dangles → axe violation → fails AC-7. Hand-rolling gives clean ARIA + full panel-decoupling (AC-7, §1). The veneer (flat array API, cx() BEM, import './Tabs.css', JSDoc, exported types) still mirrors Dropdown.**
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: A (wrap Radix + strip `aria-controls`): fights Radix internals, version-fragile post-render override, residual axe-violation risk vs AC-7. C (share one `Tabs.Root` with panels): consumers don't exist (§6 OOS), couples the primitive, breaks decoupled mandate.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Caller owns `activeId`; component holds zero internal active state; `onChange(id)` emits the request
key_design_decisions.why: Matches §7 constraint + the 001 Dropdown/Modal controlled precedent (`open`/`onOpenChange`); consistent, not a departure

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Automatic — Arrow/Home/End move selection immediately and fire `onChange`
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Manual activation (move focus, activate on Enter/Space) — §6 OOS
Spec-locked; keeps the descriptor a plain serializable shape; mirrors Dropdown's primitive-typed descriptor fields

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Optional `actions?: React.ReactNode`, rendered right-aligned at strip end, outside the `tablist` element
key_design_decisions.why: AC-8; `React.ReactNode` slot consistent with Dropdown's `trigger` prop
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Render-prop / render-slot actions option (discovery option C) — §6 OOS

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: When `activeId` matches no enabled tab (no match / empty array / all-disabled), render no `aria-selected` tab and auto-select nothing; roving tabindex falls back to the first enabled tab as the single tab-stop
key_design_decisions.why: AC-10; selection-only means the component never auto-picks. Single-state guard, no status enum introduced
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Auto-selecting the first enabled tab on no-match — contradicts AC-10

file_impact.what_changes: The `Tabs` primitive: exported `TabDescriptor` + `TabsProps` types, hand-rolled `role="tablist"` render from `tabs[]`, controlled `activeId`/`onChange`, roving tabindex + Arrow/Home/End/wrap + disabled-skip, no-selection guard, optional right-aligned `actions` slot, JSDoc citing each AC (Dropdown precedent)
Semantic BEM classes bound to `tokens.css` custom properties; no inline styles; `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` guard for any transition
Register a `Tabs` section rendering both the request-pane (6-tab) and response-pane (4-tab) sets for manual visual fidelity vs `design/reference.html`

Vitest + Testing Library interaction tests: click→onChange-once (AC-5), arrow/Home/End wrap (AC-6), disabled-skip + no-onChange-on-disabled (AC-9), no-selection guard (AC-10), tablist/tab roles + aria-selected (AC-7)
Playwright CT real-browser focus/keyboard fidelity: roving tabindex single tab-stop, focus movement, keyboard activation; assert zero axe a11y violations (AC-7)

doc_impact.what_changes: Add `Tabs` to the molecules sublayer inventory; note it hand-rolls its a11y engine (the documented exception to the "wrap Radix" rule)
Add a `Tabs` term (controlled selection-only tab-strip primitive)

risks.risk: Dangling `aria-controls` → axe violation → AC-7 fails (the core Radix-coupling tension)
risks.mitigation: Resolved by the Option B decision — the hand-rolled tablist emits no `aria-controls`; assert zero axe a11y violations in the CT test against AC-7's roles / `aria-selected` / roving-tabindex

risks.risk: Larger interaction-test surface — reimplemented roving tabindex + Arrow/Home/End/wrap + disabled-skip must be covered (the `verify_cost=Med` discovery flagged)
risks.mitigation: Derive the test matrix directly from AC-5/6/9/10 (click-once, arrow-wrap, Home/End, disabled-skip, no-onChange-on-disabled, no-selection guard); split Vitest interaction vs Playwright CT focus/keyboard per the 001 pattern

risks.risk: Hand-rolled roving tabindex regresses focus management vs Radix's tested implementation
risks.mitigation: Follow the WAI-ARIA APG Tabs pattern exactly (single tab-stop; `tabindex=0` on active/first-enabled, `-1` elsewhere); cover with Playwright CT focus assertions

risks.risk: Visual drift from `design/reference.html` at request-pane (6-tab) and response-pane (4-tab) widths
risks.mitigation: Register in PrimitivesDemo; manual visual compare at both widths; overflow handling is explicitly §6 OOS (do not solve here)

specialist_consultation.sub_question: Resolve selection-only vs Radix `aria-controls` coupling (A wrap+strip vs B hand-roll vs C share-root); author Layer Map / Key Design Decisions / Risk / Constitution rows
specialist_consultation.input_summary: Pick B (hand-roll `role=tablist`): Radix `Tabs.Trigger` deterministically emits `aria-controls` to a sibling `Content`; with no Content (selection-only) it dangles → axe → fails AC-7. Hand-roll gives clean ARIA + decoupling; veneer still mirrors Dropdown. Flagged DEPARTURE from §6.3 "wrap Radix" precedent, accepted. No §6 escalations.

dependencies: - No new packages. radix-ui ^1.1.3 stays a dependency (used by Dropdown/Modal/Toast); the hand-rolled `Tabs` introduces no second accessibility library, so AC-3 (`grep -q '"radix-ui"' package.json` + no second a11y lib) is satisfied at the dependency level. Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event + Playwright CT are already configured (feature 001). No new env vars or services.
